posts.author: Tailgunnerjoe
posts.title: The H1/H4 visa scam exposed

twitter.content:
A major loophole is being exploited in Texas

When Indians come over on a H-1B Visa, it’s not just them

They are then able to put in H-4 Visas and bring their kids and spouse

Their kids are then enrolled in American schools as residents not international students, this bypasses

posts.raw_content:
I have spent considerable time reflecting on this, and it is time for an honest discussion. The continued presence of Asians and Indians in America, including those born here across multiple generations, cannot be justified by any honest reading of history, blood, or national survival.

The Chinese who built the railroads were never meant to stay. They arrived as temporary contract laborers for specific projects. Congress recognized this reality and passed the Chinese Exclusion Act of 1882 to ensure they could not settle permanently or gain citizenship. That law was a deliberate and correct statement that Chinese people had no place as permanent residents of the United States. They remained and their descendants became citizens only because the young nation lacked the capacity for mass removal and because later political interests exploited birthright citizenship as a loophole. With serious efforts now underway to restore the original meaning of citizenship, we must confront the truth: if we never intended the Chinese to remain, there is no logical reason to accept Japanese, Koreans, Indians, or any other Asian groups either. Their presence was never part of the American founding project.

Asians and Indians possess no ancestral right to this land. Their forebears played no role in our wars of independence or territorial expansion. They did not found our cities or own the soil. Even fourth- and fifth-generation Asian Americans remain culturally and genetically distinct from the European peoples who created this nation. They now compete directly with American children for elite jobs in technology, medicine, and academia; displacing the descendants of those who built everything worth having. Politeness and high average intelligence do not grant them a claim to what was never theirs. They are extracting wealth and opportunity from a country that was never intended for them.

Paper citizenship granted through later loopholes and mass immigration waves does not override these fundamental realities. If we are serious about heritage, sovereignty, and the long-term survival of the American people as a European-descended nation, then Asians and Indians must be removed. Citizenship should be stripped wherever possible, and they should be repatriated to their ancestral homelands. Only core European Americans and those with genuine European or Spanish blood ties should have a permanent place here. This is not hatred. It is the consistent application of history and the recognition that some groups were never meant to become part of the American story.

link_metadata.description: UPDATE: Georgia State Senator Greg Dolezal has filed an emergency lawsuit to allow poll watchers and State Election Board observers into “the bunker,” where Georgia’s statewide vote totals are received and published. “Denying members of the State Election Board access to the Election Night Reporting Room is outrageous,” Georgia Republican Party Chairman Josh McKoon said, reacting to news of how the election results of this week’s primary election will be aggregated.
